title: Managed Web/API Hosting on DigitalOcean App Platform
description: Deploy APIs and background workers on DigitalOcean App Platform with automatic Git-driven deployments, managed database add-ons, and built-in monitoring. Predictable usage-based pricing; suited to teams wanting a simpler operational model than AWS/Azure without sacrificing managed infrastructure.

cost.costNotes: No free tier; minimum $5/month for Basic tier (512MB RAM, 1 vCPU). Predictable fixed pricing makes budgeting straightforward.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min $5/month represents the Basic tier (512MB RAM, 1 vCPU) suitable for prototypes, development environments, or low-traffic APIs. Max $100/month represents a typical production setup with 2-3 Professional tier apps ($12/month each) plus a managed database ($15-25/month for basic PostgreSQL) and potentially a static site component. For reference: Basic=$5, Professional=$12, Production=$50 per component. Unlike AWS/GCP with granular pricing, DigitalOcean uses fixed tiers making costs highly predictable but with no free tier option.
provenance.adoptionCost: Low adoption cost due to straightforward implementation. Git-based deployment requires minimal configuration (app spec YAML file), automatic builds from GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket, and managed infrastructure eliminates DevOps overhead. Estimated 4-8 hours for initial setup including: connecting repository, configuring environment variables, setting up build commands, and understanding deployment workflows. Simpler than Kubernetes ($3000+) or custom AWS ECS setups ($1500+), comparable to Heroku or Render. Main effort is in application preparation rather than platform learning.
provenance.licenseCost: No platform licensing fees or subscription costs beyond usage-based pricing. DigitalOcean App Platform charges only for the compute/database tiers selected with no additional platform fees, enterprise minimums, or vendor lock-in costs. All platform features (Git integration, CI/CD, monitoring, autoscaling) are included in the tier pricing. No proprietary tooling required - standard Git workflows and open-source buildpacks.

provides.reasoning: This pattern specifically provides managed hosting for APIs on DigitalOcean App Platform, handling infrastructure, deployments, and scaling automatically.
DigitalOcean App Platform includes monitoring capabilities out of the box, which is a concrete capability this pattern provides.
The pattern explicitly mentions automatic deployments from Git, which is a key capability it provides.
The description specifically mentions managed databases as part of what this pattern provides through the DigitalOcean platform.
The pattern explicitly states it can deploy background workers, not just APIs, making this a distinct capability.
The pattern specifically provides Git-based deployment integration, which is a concrete workflow capability.
DigitalOcean App Platform is fundamentally a PaaS offering that abstracts infrastructure management, which is the core capability pattern being used.
The pattern emphasizes a $5/month starter tier with predictable pricing as a key characteristic, making cost predictability a concrete capability it provides.

requires.reasoning: Since the pattern uses automatic deployments from Git, it requires the application code to be in a Git repository.
DigitalOcean App Platform deploys applications as containers, so the application needs to be containerizable or conform to supported buildpacks.

availability.reason: DigitalOcean App Platform is publicly available

supports_nfr.reason: DigitalOcean App Platform on paid tiers provides up to 99.99% availability with managed infrastructure and load balancing.
Managed PaaS with container orchestration and platform routing introduces minimum 50ms p95 latency overhead.
Platform overhead, autoscaling delays, and cold starts result in minimum 200ms p99 latency floor.
Git-based automatic deployments with health checks enable rapid rollbacks in 2-3 minutes.
Platform scales to approximately 500K jobs/hour on higher tiers with multiple worker instances.
Managed PaaS platform with moderate scaling limits supports up to 1000 read QPS for cost-optimized hosting.
Write throughput limited to 500 QPS due to managed platform resource constraints and cost-optimized tier sizing.
App Platform supports all standard authentication mechanisms through application code and reverse proxy configuration.
DigitalOcean provides GDPR-compliant infrastructure and data processing agreements with EU data residency options.
DigitalOcean offers HIPAA Business Associate Agreements for healthcare applications requiring compliance.
Platform supports CCPA compliance through data residency controls and application-level data management features.
SOX compliance can be achieved through audit logging, access controls, and change management processes.

supports_constraints.reason: DigitalOcean App Platform is a specific cloud provider but abstracts infrastructure details for cloud-agnostic deployments.
App Platform supports these languages natively through buildpacks and custom Dockerfiles for containerized workloads.
Platform hosts web applications, REST/GraphQL APIs, and background workers as long-running services.
Pattern specifically requires digitalocean-app-platform as the backend provider
Caching can be implemented using managed Redis add-ons or in-memory application caching as needed.
Async messaging can be integrated through managed message queues or third-party services connected to the platform.
AI inference workloads can run on App Platform workers or integrate with external AI services via API.
Multi-tenancy is implemented at application level with support for various isolation patterns through database design.
App Platform is designed for long-running services and APIs, not ephemeral batch jobs or scheduled tasks.
Distributed transactions can be implemented using saga patterns or two-phase commit protocols at application level.
Platform is optimized for request-response patterns, not continuous real-time streaming or high-frequency data flows.
Vector search can be integrated through managed Postgres with pgvector extension or external vector database services.
Document stores like MongoDB can be used through managed database add-ons or external MongoDB Atlas integration.
Key-value stores like Redis are available through managed database add-ons on App Platform or external services.
Graph databases can be integrated through external managed services or self-hosted instances connected to the platform.
Time-series databases can be integrated through external services or Postgres with TimescaleDB extension support.
OLTP workloads are well-supported through managed PostgreSQL and MySQL databases with standard transactional guarantees.
OLAP workloads can be handled through read replicas or integration with external analytical database services.

instance_size.description: Instance tier: basic ($5-12/mo, shared CPU), professional ($12-96/mo, dedicated CPU), dedicated (custom pricing, isolated resources)

instance_count.description: Number of instances to run. Multiple instances provide high availability and load distribution.

auto_scaling.description: Enable automatic horizontal scaling based on CPU/memory usage. Available on professional tier and above.

database_type.description: Managed database to provision: PostgreSQL/MySQL ($15+/mo), Redis ($15+/mo), MongoDB ($15+/mo), or none for external database

deployment_trigger.description: Deployment method: automatic on git push, manual via dashboard, or programmatic via API

warn_nfr.reason: At very low QPS (<1 req/s), managed PaaS overhead and $5/month cost may not be justified compared to simpler serverless options.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas--digitalocean-app-platform: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (<1). For such low traffic, serverless options may be more cost-effective than $5/month PaaS.

warn_nfr.reason: Approaching the 1000 QPS upper limit where the platform may struggle to scale cost-effectively compared to dedicated infrastructure.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas--digitalocean-app-platform: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (>800). Approaching platform limits (1000 QPS); consider IaaS or higher-performance cloud for better cost/performance at scale.

warn_nfr.reason: For availability targets above 99.9%, the cost-optimized PaaS tier may not provide adequate infrastructure redundancy without significant additional cost.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas--digitalocean-app-platform: availability target is {actual} (>99.9%). Cost-optimized PaaS may require higher-tier plans or multi-region setup to reliably achieve this SLA.
